Damian Hinds
MP for East Hampshire
In the Community

Surgeries, advice and public meetings

  • Tweet

The role of an MP

Members of Parliament are elected to the House of Commons to represent the interests and concerns of all the people who live in their constituency, whether they voted for them at the General Election or not. They are only able to deal with issues raised by people who live in their constituency, called constituents.

MPs consider and vote on legislation and use their position to ask government ministers questions about current issues.

They split their time between working in Parliament and working in the constituency. In Parliament, MPs spend their time fighting for the interests of their constituents, attending debates, scrutinising and voting on legislation, and attending meetings. In the constituency, MPs hold advice surgeries for their constituents to come and talk to them about local issues and problems, attend meetings and community events, as well as visiting local organisations and businesses.

Members of Parliament are able to help with all matters for which Parliament or central government is responsible.

Click here for the full list of government departments, agencies and public bodies.

When a constituent writes to their MP, the MP will then write to the relevant department or official or the Minister involved. Many problems are solved in this way.

Alternatively, if a constituent is happy for the issue to be made public, an MP can ask an oral or written question, secure a debate or petition Parliament.

 

Surgeries

Damian holds regular advice surgeries in East Hampshire on Friday afternoons. Each appointment is 15 minutes long and must be booked ahead of time. To make an appointment, please email the office using the details below.

You will need to provide your full name, full address with postcode and contact telephone number, as parliamentary protocol prohibits Damian from acting for constituents who are represented by another MP.  Sometimes we may ask you for your date of birth to verify your identity. 

In line with parliamentary security guidance, you may be subject to a bag search on arrival.  Please do try to leave large bags and coats at home where possible as they may not be permitted in the meeting room.

You have the option of attending the surgery in person or requesting an online appointment.

Before requesting a surgery appointment please do:

  • Check whether the issue can be sorted direct (please refer to the 'when you need help' section of this page).
  • Think about whether we could start to help you more quickly if you phoned or emailed rather than waiting for the next available surgery appointment.  

Please note that Members of Parliament are not permitted to intervene in any matters of a legal nature and cannot offer legal advice.  Members of Parliament can usually only assist with issues that are the responsibility of the UK Parliament. Often the right person to contact will be a local councillor, Citizens Advice or an ombudsman. They are unlikely to be able to assist with private disputes.

MPs do not have any jurisdiction over local Council decisions, so please contact your local Councillor (see below) if you wish to have the assistance of an elected representative in pursuing an issue with a local Council.

The full list of services provided by Hampshire County Council can be found here.

The full list of services provided by East Hampshire District Council can be found here.

The full list of services provided by Basingstoke and Deane Borough Council can be found here. Town Council websites are also useful guides to local services: 

Petersfield Town Council

Alton Town Council

Councillors

Find your District Councillor (East Hampshire District Council)

Find your Borough Councillor (Basingstoke and Deane Borough Council)

Find your County Councillor (Hampshire County Council)

Alternatively, you can find your councillor by entering your postcode here.

 

Open Q&A sessions / community meetings

Damian host regular open discussion forums in the community for all residents living in the East Hampshire constituency. This is an opportunity to raise any concerns with Damian direct and debate local issues in a relaxed and friendly environment.  These tend to take place on a Thursday evening in a community venue.  Details are published beforehand on this website and in the local newspaper.  If you would like to attend the next community event, please click here.

A full list of Damian's upcoming public meetings can be found on the homepage. 

 

Housing enquiries

Many people write to Damian about issues to do with housing. These can often be highly stressful and complex situations to navigate, and knowing where to start can be a challenge in itself.   

We always suggest that the best place to start is Hampshire HomeChoice.  This sets out the steps you need to take to register for social housing as well as the rules the local authorities (in this case East Hampshire District Council and Basingstoke and Deane Borough Council), follow to ensure that the available accommodation is allocated fairly and transparently. 

As an MP, Damian has no role or powers in relation to housing, as this is all managed by not-for-profit housing associations, but he can always make enquiries about a case or offer advice based on past experience with constituents in a similar situation. 

 

Education - Special Educational Needs and Disabilities (SEND) 

Like housing, Damian receive many emails about SEND provision for children and how to access it.  Responsibility for SEND sits with Hampshire County Council so while we can't intervene in the formal process, we can direct individuals to the correct person or team within the authority who can hopefully help with your particular situation.  

There is a wealth of information on the following websites: Hampshire SENDIASS, IPSEA, SOS!SEN or HPCN.

 

 

 

Work experience

Due to the sensitive nature of the work carried out by Damian's office, we do not currently offer work experience placements.

However, young people interested in politics can arrange to sit in on a chamber session in the House of Commons or the House of Lords, or a committee debate on any given day. Please visit our visiting Westminster section for more information.

Damian occasionally runs a 'Taste of Parliament' event for sixth form politics students living and studying in East Hampshire.  This normally takes place over a two day period in the October half term, when Parliament is still sitting.  As well as an opportunity to have a Q&A with Damian, the students will meet the teams from across Parliament who help support MPs in their day to day work.  This includes Hansard, the policy research unit and the Parliamentary archives.   If you are interested in attending a future 'Taste of Parliament' event, please get in touch so that you can be added to the waiting list.

 

 

Email Damian

 

 

Community Support

Citizens' Advice have great expertise on benefits matters and debt advice and have offices in Alton and Petersfield.  There are dedicated websites for East Hampshire and Basingstoke as well as an online help and information resource: Advice Guide.

Connect to Support Hampshire provides advice to those wishing to live independently in the community.

Alton Community Support Directory signposts local organisations that can support health and well being. 

There is lots of information on the East Hampshire District Council website about the support services available to veterans living in East Hampshire.

When you need help

Benefits calculator - Benefits calculators - GOV.UK (www.gov.uk)

Child Maintenance Service - 0800 171 2345

New benefit claims - Benefits - GOV.UK (www.gov.uk) - 0800 055 6688

Job Seekers Allowance, Income Support, Incapacity Benefit, Employment and Support Allowance - 0800 169 0310

Maternity Allowance - 0800 169 0283

Disability Living Allowance - 0800 121 4600 

Personal Independence Payment enquiries - 0800 121 4433

National Jobcentre enquiries (e.g. to make or change appointments) - 0800 169 0190 

Universal Credit - 0800 328 5644

Tax Credit Helpline - 0300 200 3300

Pensions Claim Line  - 0845 300 1084

Retirement Services (including pensions) - 0800 731 0469

Find an Ombudsman

Other useful resources

Police - non emergency 101
NHS - non emergency 111

Find your local neighbourhood policing team

Police and Crime Commissioner for Hampshire

Various immigration contact numbers are listed here
